title: Reproducible Repository, Toolchain, and Dependency Stewardship · Product Forensics
summary: A preserved decision record for reproducible repository, toolchain, and dependency stewardship contains one plausible evidence, scope, design, implementation, evaluation, or ownership defect. Find the first unsupported claim before changing the product. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in reproducible healthy project repository and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal deb…
objectives: Establish purpose, setup, architecture, commands, contribution, conduct, support, security, license, and ownership documentation.
Pin supported runtime, package manager, compiler, formatter, linter, type checker, test runner, and build behavior with compatibility evidence.
Evaluate dependency need, maintenance, provenance, license, advisories, transitive cost, API stability, and replacement or removal path.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# personal-project-1 cumulative independent product evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic evidence-contract review only.
# They never contact participants or services, execute commands, deploy, or claim live conformance.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: personal-project-1
stakeholder: [replace with affected people and accountable decision owner]
decision: [replace with the decision this evidence changes]
problem-evidence: [replace with observed need, counterevidence, and uncertainty]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
security: [replace with threat, authorization, abuse, response, and residual risk]
privacy: [replace with purpose, minimization, retention, access, correction, and deletion]
test-strategy: [replace with behavior, changed case, fault, accessibility, security, and recovery]
release-evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, inventory, provenance, validation, and rollback]
recovery: [replace with backup, clean restore, reconciliation, communication, and ownership]
portfolio-proof: [replace with contribution, limitations, counterexample, and unseen defense]
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research, toolchain, deployment, and review environments
evidence:

steps.title: Inspect the failure record · Debug Repository Contract
steps.instruction:
debug case — A promising product works only in a warm local directory with floating dependencies, an exposed sample token, unclear license, and no recovery anchor. The debug variation 6 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Establish purpose, setup, architecture, commands, contribution, conduct, support, security, license, and ownership documentation.

Inspect the Repository Contract trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Inspect the failure record · Debug Repository Contract.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for establish purpose, setup, architecture, commands, contribution, conduct, support, security, license, and ownership documentation. For Inspect the failure record · Debug Repository Contract, keep Repository Contract connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: For Repository Contract, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

hints: For Inspect the failure record · Debug Repository Contract, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Repository Contract precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Inspect the failure record · Debug Repository Contract, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A promising product works only in a warm local directory with floating dependencies, an exposed sample token, unclear license, and no recovery anchor. The debug variation 6 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Establish purpose, setup, architecture, commands, contribution, conduct, support, security, license, and ownership documentation.

For Inspect the failure record · Debug Repository Contract, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: A README installation snippet is a complete repository contract.

stimulus.title: Repository Contract case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: A promising product works only in a warm local directory with floating dependencies, an exposed sample token, unclear license, and no recovery anchor. The debug variation 6 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Establish purpose, setup, architecture, commands, contribution, conduct, support, security, license, and ownership documentation.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: A README installation snippet is a complete repository contract.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Establish purpose, setup, architecture, commands, contribution, conduct, support, security, license, and ownership documentation. Artifact under review: reproducible healthy project repository.

options.text: The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample. That claim does not survive the inspect the failure record evidence boundary.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.
The record names the assumption, applies Repository Contract, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Clean Reproduction
steps.instruction:
Append a pp1-clean-reproduction-21gdbh: manifest entry with concrete verify plus failure, owner, or repair evidence.

Evidence target: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Clean Reproduction.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Clean Reproduction into observable performance evidence. content.code:
pp1-clean-reproduction-21gdbh:
  verify: [replace with a deterministic check]
  failure: [replace with the rejected condition]
  repair: [replace with a local reproduction and correction path]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The structural check is only one gate. The following changed-case decision and explanation must also hold.

steps.title: Publish the incident record · Debug Clean Reproduction
steps.instruction:
Write the debug evidence note for Clean Reproduction: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A promising product works only in a warm local directory with floating dependencies, an exposed sample token, unclear license, and no recovery anchor. The debug variation 4 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Prove clone-to-gates-to-build behavior in a clean environment and record exact revision, lock, tool, artifact, and failure evidence.

Evidence target: Publish the incident record · Debug Clean Reproduction.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Publish the incident record · Debug Clean Reproduction, keep Clean Reproduction connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Prove clone-to-gates-to-build behavior in a clean environment and record exact revision, lock, tool, artifact, and failure evidence.
